fills: ~302 trace reconstruction (4.1.1.2 + 4.1.2.b): one learner's full decision-chain + 5-layer column exhibit

chain_length_distribution.sql: WITH per AS (SELECT user_id, count(*) AS n FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE experiment_run_id='run-d2154070-b6e4-4abe-94e6-a2e6ccfbefc1' GROUP BY user_id) SELECT count(*), sum(n), min(n), max(n), avg(n), percentile_disc(p) ... FROM per
chain_length_distribution.note: There are 93 distinct learners (user_id) in the seal run, each replayed from the Junyi-graph external log. Chains are long and right-skewed (mean 1040 >> median 563). The thesis '~302' figure corresponds to a mid-lower learner, near the p10-p25 band.

representative_learner_near_302.selection: closest single learner to the ~302 target. |313-302|=11 < next-closest |288-302|=14 (run-94a3b8ba::ex_junyi_graph_91712, n=288).
representative_learner_near_302.sql: SELECT user_id, count(*) FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE experiment_run_id='run-d2154070-...' GROUP BY user_id ORDER BY abs(count(*)-302) LIMIT 5
representative_learner_near_302.userid_prefix_note: Every user_id carries a 'run-94a3b8ba-...::' prefix. This is the V1-runtime lineage prefix inherited on this Kalman-overlay re-seal (the live IDs from the original V1 run-94a3b8ba runtime). It is the same inheritance documented in KALMAN_OVERLAY_GOVERNANCE_SCOPING.md.

five_layer_chain_definition.L3_canonical: canonical_mastery_after -- the SHIPPED canonical mastery (this seal: = kalman_mastery_after exactly, ensemble_weight_kalman=1.0)
five_layer_chain_definition.L4_mastery_after: mastery_after -- the 3-learner ensemble runtime synthesis inherited from V1 (NOT the canonical on this seal)
five_layer_chain_definition.L5_policy_score: policy_score is NULL on this offline-replay seal (live-loop-only column); the available decision-time scalar is policy_multiplier (populated 96727/96727). arm_selected also populated.
five_layer_chain_definition.policy_score_unavailable_reason: SELECT count(*) FILTER (WHERE policy_score IS NOT NULL) FROM experiment_trajectories WHERE experiment_run_id='run-d2154070-...' = 0. policy='external_log:junyi_2015_graph' confirms offline external-log replay; policy_score is only written on the live recommend/attempt loop.

exhibit_reading.canonical_is_kalman: ew_kalman=1.0 and ew_bayesian=ew_lyapunov=0.0 on every row, and canonical_mastery_after == kalman_mastery_after exactly. This is the seal's anchor config: canonical mastery = single Kalman (m=m_K); the ensemble is retained only for sigma^2 (variance), not for the point mastery.
exhibit_reading.canonical_vs_mastery_after_diverge: canonical (m_K) saturates faster than the inherited 3-learner mastery_after (e.g. row 1: 0.6667 vs 0.3000; row 8: 0.9500 vs 0.7674). The mastery_after column is the V1-runtime 3-learner ensemble synthesis -- it is the column the headline AUC and the JT/ADC governance were inherited from, NOT the shipped canonical m_K.
exhibit_reading.governance_inheritance_caveat: FRAMING: the JT contributions (L1) and ensemble weights (L2) shown here -- and all governance/ADC magnitudes downstream -- were inherited from the V1 3-learner runtime, even though the seal restates canonical mastery as m_K. Per KALMAN_OVERLAY_GOVERNANCE_SCOPING.md (research_validation/KALMAN_OVERLAY_GOVERNANCE_SCOPING.md), the JT-magnitude artifacts (Tabel 4.9/4.10, Gambar 4.28/4.29) reflect 3-learner-driven governance; the load-bearing claims (transfer, ADC verdicts, AUC competitiveness) are robust to the m_K-vs-3-learner choice.
exhibit_reading.auditability: deterministic_inputs_hash present on all 15 rows (and all 313 of this learner / all 96,727 of the run) -- the chain is reconstructable and replay-verifiable. See pass14.
